title: chaetogastra brittoniana
intro: Chaetogastra brittoniana, also known as Britton's ragwort, is a plant species in the family Asteraceae. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Britton's ragwort has been used to treat various health issues, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its health benefits of reducing inflammation and improving immune function. Traditionally, the plant has been prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or infused oil, which can be used to make topical creams and salves for skin conditions and wounds.
